Class AbstractSkill

java.lang.Object
net.sylphian.minecraft.skills.skill.AbstractSkill
All Implemented Interfaces:
Skill, Watchable, org.bukkit.event.Listener
Direct Known Subclasses:
CookingSkill, FishingSkill
public abstract class AbstractSkill extends Object implements Skill, org.bukkit.event.Listener, Watchable
Base implementation of Skill providing id, display name, ability registration, and default listener registration.

Subclasses implement their event logic as @EventHandler methods directly on the class, and register abilities via addAbility(Ability) in their constructor. Override registerListeners(Plugin, SkillsAPI) to perform one-time setup (e.g. reading a config snapshot) before calling super.registerListeners(owningPlugin, api), which stores skillsApi and registers this as a Bukkit listener.

    @Override
    public void registerListeners(Plugin owningPlugin, SkillsAPI api) {
        this.config = MyConfig.from(plugin.getConfig());
        super.registerListeners(owningPlugin, api);
    }

  • Field Details

    • skillsApi

      protected SkillsAPI skillsApi
      Available to subclass @EventHandler methods and helpers once registerListeners(Plugin, SkillsAPI) has been called.

  • Constructor Details

    • AbstractSkill

      protected AbstractSkill(String id, String displayName)
      Parameters:
      id - unique lowercase identifier, e.g. "mining"
      displayName - player-facing name, e.g. "Mining"

  • Method Details

    • getId

      public String getId()
      Specified by:
      getId in interface Skill
      Returns:
      the unique lowercase identifier for this skill, e.g. "mining"

    • getDisplayName

      public String getDisplayName()
      Specified by:
      getDisplayName in interface Skill
      Returns:
      the player-facing display name, e.g. "Mining"

    • addAbility

      protected void addAbility(Ability ability)
      Registers an ability with this skill. Call from the subclass constructor for each ability the skill unlocks, in ascending level order.
      Parameters:
      ability - the ability to register

    • getAbilities

      public List<Ability> getAbilities()
      Description copied from interface: Skill
      Returns the abilities unlocked as the player levels up this skill. The default returns an empty list; override via addAbility(Ability).
      Specified by:
      getAbilities in interface Skill
      Returns:
      an unmodifiable view of this skill's abilities, in registration order

    • firePassives

      protected void firePassives(PassiveTrigger trigger, org.bukkit.entity.Player player, UUID uuid)
      Dispatches a passive trigger to every PassiveAbility registered with this skill that accepts it and whose unlock level the player meets.
      Parameters:
      trigger - the trigger token carrying event context and accumulating outputs
      player - the player the event concerns
      uuid - the player's UUID

    • registerListeners

      public void registerListeners(org.bukkit.plugin.Plugin owningPlugin, SkillsAPI api)
      Stores the skills API reference and registers this as a Bukkit listener under owningPlugin.
      Specified by:
      registerListeners in interface Skill
      Parameters:
      owningPlugin - the plugin that owns this skill's listeners
      api - the skills API for XP, cooldowns, and buff tracking

    • onWatcherQuit

      public void onWatcherQuit(org.bukkit.event.player.PlayerQuitEvent event)
      Removes any debug watch session where the quitting player is the watcher. Prevents trace messages being sent to a disconnected admin.

    • watch

      public void watch(UUID playerUuid, org.bukkit.command.CommandSender watcher)
      Description copied from interface: Watchable
      Registers an admin to receive debug event traces for a player's skill events. Replaces any existing watcher for that player.
      Specified by:
      watch in interface Watchable
      Parameters:
      playerUuid - the player whose events to watch
      watcher - the admin to send trace output to

    • unwatch

      public void unwatch(UUID playerUuid)
      Description copied from interface: Watchable
      Removes the debug watcher for a player.
      Specified by:
      unwatch in interface Watchable
      Parameters:
      playerUuid - the player to stop watching

    • isWatched

      public boolean isWatched(UUID playerUuid)
      Description copied from interface: Watchable
      Returns true if a debug watcher is registered for this player.
      Specified by:
      isWatched in interface Watchable
      Parameters:
      playerUuid - the player's UUID
      Returns:
      true if the player is currently being watched

    • getWatcher

      @Nullable public @Nullable org.bukkit.command.CommandSender getWatcher(UUID playerUuid)
      Description copied from interface: Watchable
      Returns the admin watching this player's skill events, or null if none.
      Specified by:
      getWatcher in interface Watchable
      Parameters:
      playerUuid - the player's UUID
      Returns:
      the watching admin, or null

    • sendTrace

      protected void sendTrace(UUID uuid, TraceReport report)
      Renders one trace block to the watching admin in the standard layout shared by every skill: a header line, the per-ability contribution lines, then any result lines. Does nothing if the player is not being watched.
      Parameters:
      uuid - the target player's UUID
      report - the structured trace block to render

    • traceActiveUse

      public void traceActiveUse(UUID uuid, String subject, String ability, String detail)
      Emits a standard one-line Active trace block when the player is being watched. Active abilities call this from onActivate so their use appears in the watch trace, the way passive contributions appear as [Passive] lines.
      Parameters:
      uuid - the activating player's UUID
      subject - the player name shown in the line
      ability - the ability's display name
      detail - a short MiniMessage description of what happened

    • sendTraceResult

      protected void sendTraceResult(UUID uuid, String label, String value)
      Sends a single standalone result line in the standard trace layout. Use for results that arrive in a later event than the block header (e.g. XP awarded after a roll).
      Parameters:
      uuid - the target player's UUID
      label - the result label
      value - the MiniMessage value string
